page: Amazon Web Services m6i.large is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. The hardware configuration features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor built on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. It provides 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, allowing 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, which translates to 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, as measured by stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the average category. However, multi-core performance is limited by the single physical core design, resulting in poor ratings on multi-core stress tests and software compilation benchmarks. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks is also constrained, and database operations show weak to poor performance characteristics.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance represents an entry-level general-purpose hardware profile. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that do not require high parallel processing or massive memory throughput. Appropriate applications include lightweight web serving, single-threaded text processing, basic serialization tasks, and development or testing environments that do not demand local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.large is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal a clear tradeoff: single-core metrics fall into the average range, whereas multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are poor. This resource density makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, single-threaded text processing, and general-purpose development tasks that do not require high parallel processing power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8375C CPU, offering moderate single-core performance for basic workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and moderate single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Nitro hypervisor architecture with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core scaling
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware accelerators
Suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded processing
